Could you check the reservation again, please?
The flight might be delayed.
She could call the hotel across the street.
There seems to be a problem with my reservation.
I was told that the room was confirmed.

I agree with you.
I disagree. In my opinion, calm is faster.
I am not sorry for complaining. It is not rude. It is information.
Good evening, madam. Welcome to the Hotel Serrano. Do you have a reservation with us tonight?
Good evening. Yes, I do, Oliveira. I booked a double room with a view, back in March.
Oliveira, one moment, please. Oh. I am very sorry, madam. Your name is here, but the screen says the room was given to another family this afternoon. And we are full tonight.
I am sorry, I did not catch that. Could you say it again, more slowly, please?
Of course. Your room is not free. Another family is sleeping in it. I think we are double-booked. I am very sorry.
Then there seems to be a problem. I booked the room in March, and I was told that it was confirmed. I have the email here. Could you check it again, please?
I am checking. You are right, madam. Your confirmation is here, in the system. But the room is gone, and I am alone at the desk tonight. I do not know what I should do.
I understand. It is not your fault. But I am not going to sleep in the street tonight. I would like to speak to the manager, please.
She is coming now, madam. And thank you for being calm. The gentleman before you shouted at me for ten minutes, and I could not think at all.
Nobody sleeps better after shouting. What could the manager do for us tonight?

She might have the room we keep on the fourth floor. And she could call the hotel across the street: she knows the manager there. Please sit down, and I am going to bring you two coffees. Again, our apology.
I was told that it was confirmed. Could you check again, please?
I think we were overcharged. We did not order this.
What could you do for us tonight?

Good evening, ladies and gentlemen. This is an announcement for the passengers on the eight o'clock flight to Lisbon. I am afraid this flight is delayed. The aircraft is still in Paris, and the new departure time might be ten fifteen tonight. I say might, because we do not know yet. We are going to give you more information at nine o'clock, and we are very sorry for the delay. Now, please listen, because the next part is important, and it is different for different people. First: you should stay near this gate. Do not go far. If the aircraft arrives early, we are going to board early, and we cannot wait for you. Second: if you have another flight after Lisbon tonight, do not wait here. You should go to desk number four now, and you should speak to an agent there. They could put you on a different flight this evening, or on the first flight tomorrow morning, and they could book a hotel room for you tonight. But you should go now, because at eleven o'clock that desk is closed. Third: a delay of more than three hours is not the same as a small delay. If we leave after eleven, you could have the right to compensation from the airline. You should keep your boarding pass, and you should keep it even after the flight, because without it you cannot ask for anything. Fourth: in ten minutes, a member of our staff is going to walk along this gate with water and sandwiches, and they are free. Please take one. On behalf of the airline, I would like to give you our apology for this evening. Thank you very much for your patience.

Hello. My name is Pilar, and I am the duty manager of this hotel, here in Madrid. Twenty-two years. I am the person you ask for when something goes wrong, so let me tell you a secret, and it is going to save you a lot of nights. The guests who shout get less. Not more. Less. I know, everybody thinks the opposite. But listen. When a man shouts at my reception desk at midnight, the first thing I feel is my heart going fast, and the second thing I do is follow the rules, exactly, word by word, because that is how I protect myself. And the rules give him nothing. Nothing at all. Now, the calm woman next to him. She says: there seems to be a problem with my reservation, I booked a double room in March, and I was told that it was confirmed. Could you check it again, please? What happens then? My hands are not shaking, my head is working, and my head knows things the rules do not know. I know we have a room on the fourth floor that we keep for the airline. I know the hotel across the street has space tonight, and I know the manager there, and I can call her. And I want to give those things to her, because she was kind to me at midnight, and because it is my job and I am good at it. So please: complain. Really, complain! It is not rude, and it is not a war. It is information, and without it I cannot fix anything. But say it calmly, say it early, and ask for me by name. And one more thing. Do not say sorry for complaining. Every night somebody says to me, I am so sorry to bother you, and then they apologize three more times. You are not bothering me. This is my job, and this is my hotel, and I want to know.
